zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Caldwell於1479年出生在法國奧維阿利也圖隆-素爾-拉利也時，他的父親John Caldwell35歲，母親Alice Alston29歲。他和Margaret Lawton至少有5個兒子。他去世於1521年在他的家鄉享年42歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Caldwell于1479年出生在法国奧維阿利也圖隆-素爾-拉利也时，他的父亲John Caldwell35岁，母亲Alice Alston29岁。他和Margaret Lawton至少有5个儿子。他去世于1521年在他的家乡享年42岁。

nameMeanings.name: Caldwell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and northern Irish: habitational name from any of several places in England and Scotland, variously spelled, that are named with Old English ceald ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’. Caldwell in North Yorkshire is one major source of the surname; Caldwell in Renfrewshire in Scotland another. Possibly also from Caldwell (Warwickshire), Caldwall (Worcestershire), Cauldwell (Bedfordshire, Derbyshire, Nottinghamshire), Caudle Green (Gloucestershire), Caudle Ditch or Cawdle Fen (Cambridgeshire), Chadwell (Essex, Hertfordshire, Leicestershire, Wiltshire), Chardwell (Essex), or Chardle Ditch (Cambridgeshire, early recorded as Kadewelle).

Irish: when not the English surname, this is an Anglicized form of Ó Fuarghuis or Ó hUarghusa ‘descendant of (F)uarghus’, a personal name whose literal sense ‘cold’ + ‘choice’ was reinterpreted as coming from fuaruisce ‘cold water’.

History: Several Caldwells emigrated from Scotland to America by way of Ireland in the 18th century. James Caldwell (1734–81), a son of settler John Caldwell, was born in Charlotte County, VA, and was a militant clergyman during the revolutionary war. Andrew Caldwell, a Scottish farmer, emigrated to North America in 1718 and started a family in Lancaster County, PA. His son David was a Presbyterian clergyman and well-known revolutionary war patriot.

variants.name: Cadle
meanings: English (Gloucestershire) and Irish, both of Norman origin: nickname from Old French cadel ‘leader’.
English: variant of   Caldwell   and of   Caudill  .
Probably also an Americanized form of German   Kadel  .

variants.name: Cardwell
meanings: English and Scottish: in northern England, the West Midlands, and southwest Scotland, this surname is a variant of Caldwell . In southwestern England it is probably a variant of Cardall, a habitational name from Cardwell in Milton Abbot (Devon). See also Cardell .

variants.name: Cadwell
meanings: (Cornwall and Devon): habitational name from one of two Devon places, either Cadewell (House) in Cockington or Caddywell in Great Torrington.

variants.name: Caudill
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English caudel, cadel, caudell(e) ‘hot fortified drink, esp. one containing spiced wine or ale’, also a thin gruel (Anglo-Norman French caudel, medieval Latin caldellum). Alternatively, a variant of Caldwell . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Cordwell
meanings: English (Lancashire): variant of Caldwell . Alternatively, in Derbyshire a habitational name from Cordwell in Holmsfield, Derbyshire, named with Old English cauld ‘cold’ + well(a) ‘spring, stream’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Caldwell was born in 1479, in Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, France, his father, John Caldwell, was 35 and his mother, Alice Alston, was 29. He had at least 5 sons with Margaret Lawton. He died in 1521, in his hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Caldwell est né en 1479, à Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, France, son père, John Caldwell, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Alice Alston, avait 29 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils avec Margaret Lawton. Il est décédé en 1521, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Caldwell 1479 in Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Caldwell 35 und seine Mutter Alice Alston 29. Er hatte mit Margaret Lawton mindestens 5 Söhne. Er starb 1521 im Alter von 42 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Caldwell è nato il nel 1479 nel Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, il suo padre, John Caldwell, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Alice Alston, aveva 29 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli con Margaret Lawton. Morì nel 1521, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Caldwell(이)가 1479년 프랑스 오버그니 앨리어 토우론-수-알리어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Caldwell(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice Alston(은)는 29세였다. 그는 Margaret Lawton(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1521년 그의 고향에서 42세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Caldwell nasceu em 1479, em Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, França, seu pai, John Caldwell, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Alice Alston, tinha 29 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos com Margaret Lawton. Ele faleceu em 1521, em sua cidade natal, com 42 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Caldwell родился в 1479 г. в местности тоулон-сур-аллиир, аллиир, аувиргн, Франция, его отцу по имени John Caldwell было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Alston, 29. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей от Margaret Lawton. Он умер в 1521 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 42 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Caldwell nació en 1479, en Toulon-sur-Allier, Allier, Auvergne, Francia, su papá, John Caldwell, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Alice Alston, tenía 29 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos con Margaret Lawton. Murió en 1521, en su pueblo, a la edad de 42 años.
